docs.title: Smart-Diffusion Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the Smart-Diffusion documentation! Smart-Diffusion is a high-performance diffusion model inference framework that provides extreme performance and flexible scheduling for AI-generated content (AIGC) workloads.

docs.title: What is Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion is built on Chitu, a high-performance LLM inference framework. It extends Chitu's capabilities to support the rapidly growing Diffusion ecosystem, providing:

  • 🚀 Extreme Performance: Advanced parallelism strategies and optimized kernels
  • 🔧 Flexible Architecture: Multiple attention backend support
  • 💾 Memory Efficiency: Low memory modes with intelligent model offloading
  • 📊 Smart Caching: Feature reuse algorithms for acceleration
  • 🎯 Simple API: Easy-to-use interface with per-request configuration

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• :material-clock-fast:{ .lg .middle } Get Started

    Install Smart-Diffusion and run your first generation in minutes

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: Installation

  • :material-book-open-variant:{ .lg .middle } User Guide

    Learn how to use Smart-Diffusion effectively

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: Basic Usage

  • :material-tune:{ .lg .middle } Performance Tuning

    Optimize your inference for speed and memory

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: Tuning Guide

  • :material-api:{ .lg .middle } API Reference

    Detailed API documentation for all components

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: API Docs

docs.title: High-Performance Inference
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion achieves superior performance through:

  • Parallelism: Context parallelism (CP), CFG parallelism, and data parallelism
  • Optimized Kernels: FlashAttention, SageAttention, SpargeAttention
  • Smart Scheduling: Efficient task management and resource utilization

docs.title: Memory Efficiency
docs.text:
Run large models on limited hardware:

  • Model Offloading: CPU offloading for DiT models and encoders
  • VAE Tiling: Reduced memory usage during decoding
  • Flexible Configuration: Adjustable memory levels (0-3)

docs.title: Feature Reuse
docs.text:
Accelerate generation with intelligent caching:

  • TeaCache: Temporal adaptive caching (CVPR24)
  • PAB: Pyramid attention broadcasting (ICLR25)

docs.title: Supported Models
docs.text:
Currently supported:

  • Wan-AI/Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B (1.3B parameters)
  • Wan-AI/Wan2.1-T2V-14B (14B parameters)
  • Wan-AI/Wan2.2-T2V-A14B (14B parameters, two-stage)

More models coming soon!

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[User Request] --> B[Task Pool]
    B --> C[Scheduler]
    C --> D[Generator]
    D --> E[Text Encoder]
    D --> F[DiT Model]
    D --> G[VAE Decoder]
    E --> H[Latent Tensors]
    F --> H
    H --> G
    G --> I[Output Video]

Smart-Diffusion follows a modular architecture:

  1. Task Management: User requests are converted to tasks and added to the task pool
  2. Scheduling: The scheduler selects pending tasks for execution
  3. Generation: The generator orchestrates the full generation pipeline:
  4. Text encoding (T5)
  5. Iterative denoising (DiT)
  6. VAE decoding
  7. Output: Generated videos are saved to disk

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
Join our community:

  • GitHub: chen-yy20/SmartDiffusion
  • Issues: Report bugs and request features
  • Discussions: Ask questions and share ideas

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Ready to get started?

  1. Install Smart-Diffusion
  2. Run your first generation
  3. Explore advanced features
  4. Read the design philosophy

Note: Smart-Diffusion is under active development. We welcome contributions and feedback!

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to Smart-Diffusion will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Comprehensive English docstrings for core modules
  • chitu_core/device_type.py - Device detection functions
  • chitu_core/global_vars.py - Configuration management
  • chitu_diffusion/backend.py - Backend and model management
  • chitu_diffusion/chitu_diffusion_main.py - Main initialization and execution
  • Enhanced README.md with:
  • Improved structure and formatting
  • Comprehensive feature descriptions
  • Detailed installation instructions with uv support
  • Usage examples and configuration guide
  • Contributing guidelines and roadmap
  • Complete documentation website using MkDocs Material
  • Installation guide
  • Quick start tutorial
  • Architecture overview
  • FAQ section
  • Configuration guide
  • GitHub Actions workflow for automatic documentation deployment
  • Support for documentation search and code highlighting

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Reorganized documentation structure with clear sections
  • Improved code documentation standards across the codebase

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release of Smart-Diffusion
  • Support for Wan-T2V series models (1.3B, 14B, A14B)
  • Multiple attention backend support:
  • FlashAttention (default)
  • SageAttention (quantized)
  • SpargeAttention (sparse)
  • Memory optimization features:
  • Low memory mode with model offloading
  • VAE tiling support
  • Multi-level memory management (0-3)
  • FlexCache system for feature reuse:
  • TeaCache strategy
  • Pyramid Attention Broadcast (PAB) strategy
  • Parallelism support:
  • Context Parallelism (CP)
  • Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) parallelism
  • Evaluation support:
  • VBench custom-mode evaluation
  • Configuration system:
  • Hydra-based configuration
  • Three-tier parameter system (Model/User/System)
  • Task management:
  • Task pool and scheduler
  • Request serialization for distributed execution
  • Distributed training support:
  • NCCL backend
  • Multi-GPU and multi-node support

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
• Data parallelism not yet implemented
  • Limited model support (Wan-T2V only)
  • Documentation incomplete in some areas

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
See Contributing Guide for how to contribute to Smart-Diffusion.

For detailed commit history, see GitHub Commits.

docs.title: Q: What GPU do I need to run Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text:
A: Smart-Diffusion requires an NVIDIA GPU with: - Compute capability 8.0+ (Ampere architecture or newer) - At least 24GB VRAM for 14B models (can use low memory mode with less) - CUDA 12.4 or higher

Supported GPUs: - A100, A10, A30 (Ampere) - H100, H20 (Hopper) - B100, B200, RTX 5090 (Blackwell)

docs.title: Q: Can I run Smart-Diffusion on AMD or Intel GPUs?
docs.text: A: Currently, Smart-Diffusion only supports NVIDIA GPUs with CUDA. Support for ROCm (AMD) and other platforms may be added in the future.

docs.title: Q: Do I need to compile from source?
docs.text: A: Not necessarily. Basic installation with FlashAttention can use pre-built wheels. However, for optimal performance with SageAttention and SpargeAttention, compilation is recommended.

docs.title: Q: How long does installation take?
docs.text: A: - Basic installation (pip/uv): 5-10 minutes - Full installation with compilation: 10-20 minutes - Depends on your internet speed and CPU cores

docs.title: Q: What if I encounter CUDA version mismatches?
docs.text: A: 1. Check your CUDA version: nvcc --version 2. Update pyproject.toml to match your CUDA version 3. For uv: uv sync -v --reinstall 4. For pip: pip install --force-reinstall torch torchvision

docs.title: Q: Which model should I use?
docs.text: A: - Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B: Fast, good for testing and lower-end GPUs - Wan2.1-T2V-14B: Best quality, requires more VRAM - Wan2.2-T2V-A14B: Advanced two-stage model with highest quality

docs.title: Q: How much VRAM do I need?
docs.text:
A: Approximate VRAM requirements:

Model Resolution Frames Min VRAM Recommended 1.3B 480x848 81 16GB 24GB 14B 480x848 81 24GB 40GB 14B 720x1280 121 40GB 80GB

Use low_mem_level to reduce VRAM usage: 

python test_generate.py infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

docs.title: Q: How can I speed up generation?
docs.text:
A: Several strategies:

  1. Use SageAttention: infer.attn_type=sage (~2x faster)
  2. Reduce inference steps: num_inference_steps=30 (was 50)
  3. Enable FlexCache: flexcache='teacache'
  4. Lower resolution: height=480, width=848
  5. Use multiple GPUs: Enable context parallelism

docs.title: Q: What's the difference between attention backends?
docs.text: A: - flash_attn: Default, accurate, good performance - sage: Quantized (INT8), ~2x faster, minimal quality loss - sparge: Sparse + quantized, ~3x faster, slight quality loss - auto: Automatically select best available

docs.title: Q: Why is the first generation slow?
docs.text:
A: The first generation includes: - Model loading - CUDA kernel compilation - Memory allocation

Subsequent generations will be much faster. Use warmup if needed.

docs.title: Q: How do I set the output path?
docs.text:
A: Set save_path in DiffusionUserParams:

user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    save_path="./my_videos/output.mp4"
)

docs.title: Q: Can I generate multiple videos at once?
docs.text:
A: Yes! Add multiple tasks to the pool:

for prompt in prompts:
    task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(
        DiffusionUserParams(prompt=prompt)
    )
    DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

docs.title: Q: How do I adjust video quality?
docs.text: A: Key parameters: - num_inference_steps: More steps = better quality (30-100) - guidance_scale: Higher = more prompt adherence (5.0-15.0) - resolution: Higher resolution = better detail

docs.title: Q: What does guidance_scale do?
docs.text: A: Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale controls how closely the model follows your prompt: - Low (3-5): More creative, less prompt adherence - Medium (7-9): Balanced (recommended) - High (10-15): Strict prompt following, may reduce creativity

docs.title: Q: I'm getting "CUDA out of memory" errors
docs.text:
A: Try these solutions in order:

  1. Enable low memory mode: 

    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

  2. Reduce resolution: 

    height=480, width=848, num_frames=61

  3. Use SageAttention (lower memory): 

    infer.attn_type=sage

  4. Close other programs using GPU

docs.title: Q: What is low_mem_level?
docs.text: A: - Level 0: All models on GPU (fastest, most VRAM) - Level 1: VAE uses tiling (slight slowdown) - Level 2: T5 encoder on CPU (moderate slowdown) - Level 3: DiT model on CPU (significant slowdown)

docs.title: Q: How do I check VRAM usage?
docs.text:
A: During generation, Smart-Diffusion logs memory usage. You can also use:

# In another terminal
nvidia-smi -l 1  # Updates every second

Or in Python: 

import torch
print(f"Allocated: {torch.cuda.memory_allocated()/1024**3:.2f} GB")
print(f"Reserved: {torch.cuda.memory_reserved()/1024**3:.2f} GB")

docs.title: Q: How do I use multiple GPUs?
docs.text:
A:

Data parallelism (future): 

torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 test_generate.py

Context parallelism: 

python test_generate.py infer.diffusion.cp_size=2

CFG parallelism (automatic if world_size >= 2 and CFG enabled)

docs.title: Q: What's the difference between CP and CFG parallelism?
docs.text: A: - Context Parallelism (CP): Splits frames across GPUs for longer videos - CFG Parallelism: Splits positive/negative prompts across 2 GPUs for faster generation

docs.title: Q: Can I combine different parallelism strategies?
docs.text:
A: Yes! For example, 4 GPUs can use: - 2 GPUs for CFG parallelism - 2 GPUs for context parallelism

# 4 GPUs total = 2 CFG × 2 CP
python test_generate.py infer.diffusion.cfg_size=2 infer.diffusion.cp_size=2

docs.title: Q: Model loading is very slow
docs.text: A: Possible causes: 1. Slow disk: Use SSD for model storage 2. Network storage: Copy model to local disk 3. Large model: 14B models take time to load

docs.title: Q: Generation stops or hangs
docs.text: A: Check: 1. CUDA errors in logs 2. Out of memory (check nvidia-smi) 3. Network issues (for distributed training) 4. Ctrl+C may be caught - wait or force kill

docs.title: Q: Video quality is poor
docs.text: A: Try: 1. Increase num_inference_steps (50-100) 2. Adjust guidance_scale (7-10) 3. Use higher resolution 4. Try different prompt phrasing 5. Use 14B model instead of 1.3B

docs.title: Q: Colors look wrong in generated video
docs.text: A: This may be a VAE decoding issue. Try: 1. Update to latest version 2. Check if issue persists with different prompts 3. Report as a bug with sample output

docs.title: Q: How does FlexCache work?
docs.text:
A: FlexCache reuses features from previous denoising steps: - TeaCache: Reuses features when they change slowly - PAB: Broadcasts high-level features to lower levels

Enables ~30-50% speedup with minimal quality loss.

docs.title: Q: Can I fine-tune models with Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text: A: Smart-Diffusion is designed for inference only. Fine-tuning should be done with training frameworks, then load checkpoints for inference.

docs.title: Q: How do I add a custom model?
docs.text: A: See Custom Models guide. Summary: 1. Create model class implementing required interface 2. Register model type 3. Create configuration file 4. Load checkpoint

docs.title: Q: What's the difference between Chitu and Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text: A: - Chitu: General LLM inference framework - Smart-Diffusion: Specialized for diffusion models (DiT), built on Chitu

docs.title: Q: How can I contribute?
docs.text: A: See Contributing Guide: - Report bugs - Suggest features - Submit pull requests - Improve documentation - Share usage examples

docs.title: Q: What's the development roadmap?
docs.text: A: Key priorities: 1. More model support (SD3, Flux, CogVideoX) 2. Better performance optimizations 3. Production serving features 4. Comprehensive benchmarks

docs.title: Q: Where can I get help not covered in FAQ?
docs.text: A: 1. Documentation: Check User Guide 2. GitHub Issues: Report bugs 3. Discussions: Ask questions

docs.title: Q: How do I report a bug?
docs.text: A: Open an issue with: - System info (GPU, CUDA version, Python version) - Installation method - Command/code that caused the error - Full error message - Expected vs actual behavior

docs.title: Q: Is there a community chat?
docs.text: A: We're considering setting up Discord/Slack. For now, use: - GitHub Discussions for questions - GitHub Issues for bugs - Pull requests for contributions

docs.title: Why Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text: 中文版

docs.title: Workload Characteristics of Diffusion
docs.text:
Diffusion inference is a full-stack compute-bound task:

  1. Sample-by-sample execution: Batching hardly improves GPU utilization; pure streaming is mandatory.
  2. Long activation sequences, yet relatively small models: Sequence Parallelism becomes the most economical and scalable dimension.
  3. In long-sequence regimes, Full Attention accounts for ~80 % of end-to-end latency: operator-level efforts must target Attention first.
  4. Activations change mildly between denoising steps: a simple, lossy Feature Cache yields instant speed-ups.

docs.title: Three Pillars: Parallelism × Kernels × Algorithms
docs.text: Each can be pursued independently, but co-design extracts the last drop of performance. (Technical deep-dives will be released incrementally—PRs welcome!)

docs.title: Service Framework for Multi-User, Multi-Task Workloads
docs.text:
We ship a long-running, hot-upgradable, horizontally-scalable Diffusion service—not a frozen script that cold-starts every time. Key idea: decompose the Diffusion pipeline into composable stages and orchestrate them with a unified scheduler:

  • Let users tune their own quality-efficiency trade-off: steps, CFG, cache ratio—all at runtime.
  • Keep all resources saturated: FLOPs are only the first bottleneck; memory, bandwidth and CPU must be fully utilized as well.

docs.title: Developer Guide
docs.text:
Thanks for joining the Smart-Diffusion open-source community! To keep code review painless, please align on the “parameter taxonomy” first:

Category Life-Cycle Location Who Can Change Best Practice Model params Static chitu_core/config/models/<model>.yaml Nobody Tied to weights; any change is UB User params Dynamic (per-request) DiffusionUserParams End user Expose necessary & sufficient knobs; avoid parameter spam System params Semi-dynamic (init-time) chitu launch args Ops/Scheduler No hot-edit after init; prevents distributed state explosion

Remember: Every extra knob costs documentation + tests + user mental model. Flexibility ≠ surface area.

docs.title: 为什么需要 Smart-Diffusion？
docs.text: English Editon

docs.title: Diffusion 任务的负载画像
docs.text:
Diffusion 推理是「全程高密度计算」的典型代表：

  1. 逐样本执行：Batch 无法带来 GPU 利用率提升，单样本流式处理就已经足够。
  2. 激活序列超长，模型反而“小”：序列并行（Context Parallelism）成为最经济、最易扩展的切分维度。
  3. 在长序列场景下，Full Attention 占端到端延迟 80 % 以上：算子级优化主要针对attention。
  4. 相邻去噪步的激活变化微弱：Feature Cache 这一“有损但简单”的 trick 就能带来立竿见影的加速。

docs.title: 三驾马车：并行 × 算子 × 算法
docs.text: 三条路线可独立落地，但灵活 Co-Design 才能榨干硬件的最后一滴油。 （技术细节将在后续章节持续更新，欢迎 PR 一起填坑。）

docs.title: 面向「多用户 × 多任务」的服务框架
docs.text:
我们交付的是可常驻、可热升级、可横向扩展的 Diffusion 服务，而非写死脚本 + 冷启动。 核心是把 Diffusion Pipeline 拆成可编排的 Stage，并用统一调度器统筹：

  • 让用户自己决定「质量-效率」天平：步数、CFG、Cache 比例一键可调。
  • 让系统资源始终饱和：算力打满只是及格线，显存、带宽、CPU 也要“物尽其用”。

docs.title: 开发指南
docs.text: 感谢加入 Smart-Diffusion 开源生态！为了让大家 Review 代码时少掉几根头发，请先把开发的三观对齐：

docs.title: 参数设计
docs.text:
参数类别 生命周期 配置位置 谁能改 最佳实践 模型参数 静态 chitu_core/config/models/<model>.yaml 无人可改 与权重强绑定，动一行就翻车 用户参数 动态（per-request） DiffusionUserParams 终端用户 暴露「必要且足够」的旋钮，拒绝大排档式刷屏 系统参数 半动态（启动时） chitu launch args 运维/调度器 初始化后禁止热插拔，防止分布式状态爆炸

记住： “灵活性” 与 “易用性” 永远在天平两端——多一个暴露参数，就多一份文档、一份测试、一份用户心智负担。

docs.title: 目录结构
docs.text: /chitu_core 中是Chitu原生提供的代码。对ServeConfig和ParallelState非必要不修改。 /chitu_diffusion 则是我们仿照Chitu逻辑搭建的diffusion框架，可以修改，但应该保持基本的结构。 * chitu_diffusion_main.py: 系统初始化、启动、关闭等主要参数 * backend.py 基于系统参数搭建的后端，储存模型，调度任务。

docs.title: API Reference - Core
docs.text: This page documents the core API of Smart-Diffusion.

docs.title: chitu_init
docs.text:
def chitu_init(args, logging_level=None)

Initialize the Chitu diffusion system.

Parameters: - args: Configuration object containing all system parameters - logging_level: Optional logging level override (default: INFO, DEBUG if CHITU_DEBUG=1)

Initializes: 1. Logging system 2. Environment variables 3. Distributed training groups 4. Model checkpoints 5. Backend, scheduler, and generator

Example:

from hydra import compose, initialize
from chitu_diffusion import chitu_init

initialize(config_path="config", version_base=None)
args = compose(config_name="wan")
args.models.ckpt_dir = "/path/to/checkpoint"

chitu_init(args)

docs.title: chitu_start
docs.text:
def chitu_start()

Mark the backend as running and ready to process tasks.

Example:

chitu_start()

docs.title: chitu_generate
docs.text:
@torch.inference_mode()
def chitu_generate()

Execute one generation step across all ranks.

Must be called in a loop until all tasks are finished. Rank 0 schedules and processes tasks, while other ranks participate in distributed computation.

Example:

while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

docs.title: chitu_terminate
docs.text:
def chitu_terminate()

Gracefully terminate the Chitu backend.

Signals all ranks to stop processing by setting the backend state to Terminated.

Example:

chitu_terminate()

docs.title: chitu_is_terminated
docs.text:
def chitu_is_terminated() -> bool

Check if the Chitu backend has been terminated.

Returns: - bool: True if terminated, False otherwise

docs.title: DiffusionUserParams
docs.text:
@dataclass
class DiffusionUserParams:
    role: str = "user"
    prompt: str = ""
    height: int = 480
    width: int = 848
    num_frames: int = 81
    fps: int = 24
    num_inference_steps: int = 50
    guidance_scale: float = 7.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    save_path: Optional[str] = None
    flexcache: Optional[str] = None

User-facing parameters for video generation.

Fields: - role: User identifier - prompt: Text description of desired video - height: Video height in pixels - width: Video width in pixels - num_frames: Number of frames to generate - fps: Frames per second - num_inference_steps: Number of denoising steps - guidance_scale: CFG scale for prompt adherence - seed: Random seed (None for random) - save_path: Output path (None for auto-generated) - flexcache: Cache strategy ('teacache', 'PAB', or None)

Example:

from chitu_diffusion.task import DiffusionUserParams

params = DiffusionUserParams(
    role="user1",
    prompt="A cat walking on grass",
    num_inference_steps=50,
    guidance_scale=7.0,
)

docs.title: DiffusionTask
docs.text:
class DiffusionTask:
    def __init__(self, user_request: DiffusionUserRequest)

    @classmethod
    def from_user_request(cls, params: DiffusionUserParams) -> "DiffusionTask"

    @classmethod
    def create_terminate_signal(cls, task_id: str) -> "DiffusionTask"

Internal task representation with buffers and status tracking.

Methods: - from_user_request(params): Create task from user parameters - create_terminate_signal(task_id): Create termination signal task

Example:

from chitu_diffusion.task import DiffusionTask, DiffusionUserParams

params = DiffusionUserParams(prompt="A cat")
task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(params)

docs.title: DiffusionTaskPool
docs.text:
class DiffusionTaskPool:
    pool: Dict[str, DiffusionTask] = {}

    @classmethod
    def add(cls, task: DiffusionTask)

    @classmethod
    def all_finished(cls) -> bool

    @classmethod
    def get_pending_tasks(cls) -> List[str]

Global task pool manager.

Methods: - add(task): Add task to pool - all_finished(): Check if all tasks are completed - get_pending_tasks(): Get list of pending task IDs

Example:

from chitu_diffusion.task import DiffusionTaskPool

DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

docs.title: Model Configuration
docs.text:
Model parameters are defined in YAML files:

# chitu_core/config/models/wan.yaml
name: Wan2.1-T2V-14B
type: diff-wan
ckpt_dir: null  # Set at runtime
source: https://huggingface.co/Wan-AI/Wan2.1-T2V-14B

transformer:
  in_channels: 16
  patch_size: [1, 2, 2]
  hidden_size: 3072
  depth: 40
  num_heads: 24
  # ... more parameters

docs.title: System Configuration
docs.text:
System parameters are set via launch arguments:

args.infer.attn_type = "sage"
args.infer.diffusion.low_mem_level = 2
args.infer.diffusion.cp_size = 1

Or via command line:

python script.py \
    infer.attn_type=sage \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

docs.title: Device Detection
docs.text:
from chitu_core.device_type import (
    get_device_name,
    is_nvidia,
    is_hopper,
    has_native_fp8
)

device = get_device_name()  # "NVIDIA H100"
if is_hopper():
    print("Using Hopper GPU")

docs.title: Global Configuration
docs.text:
from chitu_core.global_vars import get_global_args

args = get_global_args()
print(f"Model: {args.models.name}")

docs.title: Common Exceptions
docs.text:
ValueError: Invalid configuration 

# Raised when checkpoint path is not provided
args.models.ckpt_dir = None  # Raises ValueError

FileNotFoundError: Checkpoint not found 

# Raised when checkpoint files don't exist
args.models.ckpt_dir = "/invalid/path"  # Raises FileNotFoundError

RuntimeError: CUDA errors 

# Raised when running out of GPU memory
# Use low_mem_level to reduce memory usage

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
from chitu_diffusion import (
    chitu_init, chitu_start, chitu_generate, chitu_terminate
)
from chitu_diffusion.task import (
    DiffusionUserParams, DiffusionTask, DiffusionTaskPool
)
from hydra import compose, initialize

# Initialize
initialize(config_path="config", version_base=None)
args = compose(config_name="wan")
args.models.ckpt_dir = "/path/to/checkpoint"

chitu_init(args)
chitu_start()

# Create task
params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A cat walking on grass",
    height=480,
    width=848,
    num_frames=81,
    num_inference_steps=50,
)

task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(params)
DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

# Generate
while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

# Cleanup
chitu_terminate()

print(f"Done! Video: {task.buffer.save_path}")

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Backend API - DiffusionBackend details
  • Generator API - Generation pipeline
  • Task API - Task management
  • Scheduler API - Task scheduling

docs.title: Why Smart-Diffusion?
docs.text: 中文版

docs.title: Workload Characteristics of Diffusion
docs.text:
Diffusion inference is a full-stack compute-bound task:

  1. Sample-by-sample execution: Batching hardly improves GPU utilization; pure streaming is mandatory.
  2. Long activation sequences, yet relatively small models: Sequence Parallelism becomes the most economical and scalable dimension.
  3. In long-sequence regimes, Full Attention accounts for ~80 % of end-to-end latency: operator-level efforts must target Attention first.
  4. Activations change mildly between denoising steps: a simple, lossy Feature Cache yields instant speed-ups.

docs.title: Three Pillars: Parallelism × Kernels × Algorithms
docs.text: Each can be pursued independently, but co-design extracts the last drop of performance. (Technical deep-dives will be released incrementally—PRs welcome!)

docs.title: Service Framework for Multi-User, Multi-Task Workloads
docs.text:
We ship a long-running, hot-upgradable, horizontally-scalable Diffusion service—not a frozen script that cold-starts every time. Key idea: decompose the Diffusion pipeline into composable stages and orchestrate them with a unified scheduler:

  • Let users tune their own quality-efficiency trade-off: steps, CFG, cache ratio—all at runtime.
  • Keep all resources saturated: FLOPs are only the first bottleneck; memory, bandwidth and CPU must be fully utilized as well.

docs.title: Developer Guide
docs.text:
Thanks for joining the Smart-Diffusion open-source community! To keep code review painless, please align on the “parameter taxonomy” first:

Category Life-Cycle Location Who Can Change Best Practice Model params Static chitu_core/config/models/<model>.yaml Nobody Tied to weights; any change is UB User params Dynamic (per-request) DiffusionUserParams End user Expose necessary & sufficient knobs; avoid parameter spam System params Semi-dynamic (init-time) chitu launch args Ops/Scheduler No hot-edit after init; prevents distributed state explosion

Remember: Every extra knob costs documentation + tests + user mental model. Flexibility ≠ surface area.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: This document provides an overview of Smart-Diffusion's architecture and design principles.

docs.title: System Architecture
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion follows a modular, pipeline-based architecture optimized for high-performance diffusion inference:

graph TB
    subgraph "User Layer"
        A[User Request] --> B[DiffusionUserParams]
    end

    subgraph "Task Management"
        B --> C[DiffusionTask]
        C --> D[DiffusionTaskPool]
    end

    subgraph "Scheduling Layer"
        D --> E[DiffusionScheduler]
        E --> F{Task Ready?}
        F -->|Yes| G[Select Task]
        F -->|No| E
    end

    subgraph "Execution Layer"
        G --> H[Generator]
        H --> I[Text Encoding]
        H --> J[Iterative Denoising]
        H --> K[VAE Decoding]
    end

    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        I --> L[T5 Encoder]
        J --> M[DiT Model]
        K --> N[VAE]
        L --> O[DiffusionBackend]
        M --> O
        N --> O
    end

    subgraph "Acceleration Layer"
        O --> P[Attention Backend]
        O --> Q[FlexCache Manager]
        O --> R[Distributed Groups]
        P --> S[FlashAttn/Sage/Sparge]
    end

    K --> T[Output Video]

docs.title: 1. Task Management System
docs.text:
Purpose: Manage user requests and track generation progress.

Key Classes: - DiffusionUserParams: User-facing parameters for generation - DiffusionTask: Internal task representation with buffers - DiffusionTaskPool: Global task pool manager

Features: - Task serialization for distributed execution - Progress tracking - Buffer management for intermediate states

docs.title: 2. Scheduler
docs.text:
Purpose: Select and order tasks for execution.

Key Class: DiffusionScheduler

Strategy: FIFO (First-In-First-Out)

Responsibilities: - Task selection - Resource allocation - Fairness enforcement

docs.title: 3. Generator
docs.text:
Purpose: Execute the generation pipeline.

Key Class: Generator

Pipeline Stages: 1. Text Encoding: Convert prompt to embeddings using T5 2. Denoising: Iteratively denoise latent through DiT model 3. VAE Decoding: Convert latent to pixel space

Features: - Multi-stage pipeline management - Distributed communication handling - Memory-efficient processing

docs.title: 4. Backend
docs.text:
Purpose: Manage model loading, parallelism, and resources.

Key Class: DiffusionBackend

Responsibilities: - Model checkpoint loading - Distributed group initialization - Memory management - Component coordination

Components Managed: - Text encoder (T5) - DiT models (single or multi-stage) - VAE decoder - FlexCache manager - Attention backend

docs.title: 1. Static Singleton Pattern
docs.text:
The DiffusionBackend uses static class attributes to maintain global state:

class DiffusionBackend:
    model_pool = []  # Shared across all instances
    scheduler = None
    generator = None
    # ...

Rationale: Simplifies distributed coordination and resource sharing.

docs.title: 2. Factory Pattern
docs.text:
Model creation uses factory methods:

@staticmethod
def _build_model_architecture(args, attn_backend, rope_impl):
    model_type = ModelType(args.type)
    model_cls = get_model_class(model_type)
    return model_cls(...)

Rationale: Flexible model selection and instantiation.

docs.title: 3. Strategy Pattern
docs.text:
Attention backends use strategy pattern:

class DiffusionAttnBackend:
    def __init__(self, attn_type):
        if attn_type == "flash_attn":
            self.impl = FlashAttention()
        elif attn_type == "sage":
            self.impl = SageAttention()
        # ...

Rationale: Easy swapping of attention implementations.

docs.title: Generation Request Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Task as DiffusionTask
    participant Pool as TaskPool
    participant Sched as Scheduler
    participant Gen as Generator
    participant Backend as DiffusionBackend

    User->>Task: Create with params
    Task->>Pool: Add to pool

    loop Until all finished
        Sched->>Pool: Get pending tasks
        Pool-->>Sched: Return task IDs
        Sched->>Gen: Pass selected task
        Gen->>Backend: Request text encoding
        Backend-->>Gen: Return embeddings

        loop Denoising steps
            Gen->>Backend: Request DiT forward
            Backend-->>Gen: Return denoised latent
        end

        Gen->>Backend: Request VAE decoding
        Backend-->>Gen: Return video frames
        Gen->>Task: Update buffer & status
    end

    Task-->>User: Save video to disk

docs.title: Memory Hierarchy
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion manages memory across multiple levels:

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  GPU VRAM (Fastest)                 │
│  - Active DiT model                 │
│  - Activations                      │
│  - KV cache (if enabled)            │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
           ↕ (Offload)
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  CPU RAM (Fast)                     │
│  - Text encoder (low_mem_level≥2)  │
│  - Inactive DiT models (≥3)         │
│  - VAE (optional)                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
           ↕ (Swap)
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Disk (Slow)                        │
│  - Model checkpoints                │
│  - Output videos                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Memory Optimization Strategies
docs.text:
1. Model Offloading: Move unused models to CPU
  2. VAE Tiling: Process video in tiles to reduce peak memory
  3. Gradient Checkpointing: Recompute activations during backward pass
  4. Mixed Precision: Use FP16/BF16 where possible

docs.title: Parallelism Strategy
docs.text: Smart-Diffusion supports multiple parallelism dimensions:

docs.title: 1. Context Parallelism (CP)
docs.text:
Split sequence dimension across GPUs:

GPU 0: [frames 0-40]
GPU 1: [frames 41-80]

Benefits: - Handle longer sequences - Linear memory scaling

Communication: All-to-all for attention

docs.title: 2. CFG Parallelism
docs.text:
Split positive/negative prompts:

GPU 0: Positive prompt
GPU 1: Negative prompt

Benefits: - 2x speedup for CFG - No extra memory overhead

Communication: All-gather for combining predictions

docs.title: 3. Data Parallelism (Future)
docs.text:
Process multiple requests in parallel:

GPU 0: Request A
GPU 1: Request B

Benefits: - Higher throughput - Better resource utilization

docs.title: Backend Selection
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion supports multiple attention implementations:

Backend Precision Speed Memory FlashAttention FP16/BF16 1x 1x SageAttention INT8 ~2x 0.5x SpargeAttention INT8 + Sparse ~3x 0.3x

docs.title: Attention Flow with CP
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Input Sequence] --> B[Split by CP]
    B --> C[Local Attention]
    C --> D[All-to-All Comm]
    D --> E[Merge Results]
    E --> F[Output Sequence]

docs.title: FlexCache System
docs.text: FlexCache enables feature reuse across denoising steps:

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
FlexCacheManager
├── Strategy (TeaCache / PAB)
├── Cache Buffer (GPU/CPU)
└── Indexer (which layers to cache)

docs.title: Cache Decision Flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Denoising Step] --> B{Check Strategy}
    B -->|TeaCache| C{Temporal Distance}
    B -->|PAB| D{Pyramid Level}
    C -->|Close| E[Reuse Cache]
    C -->|Far| F[Recompute]
    D -->|High| E
    D -->|Low| F
    E --> G[Update Cache]
    F --> G

docs.title: Configuration Taxonomy
docs.text: Smart-Diffusion uses a three-level configuration system:

docs.title: 1. Model Parameters (Static)
docs.text:
Location: chitu_core/config/models/<model>.yaml

Content: Architecture-specific parameters (layers, heads, hidden size)

Cannot be changed after checkpoint creation.

docs.title: 2. User Parameters (Dynamic)
docs.text:
Location: DiffusionUserParams

Content: Per-request parameters (prompt, steps, CFG scale)

Can be changed for each generation request.

docs.title: 3. System Parameters (Semi-static)
docs.text:
Location: Launch arguments

Content: Parallelism, operators, memory mode

Cannot be changed after initialization (requires restart).

docs.title: Extension Points
docs.text: Smart-Diffusion is designed for extensibility:

docs.title: Adding New Models
docs.text:
1. Create model class in chitu_core/models/
  2. Register in ModelType enum
  3. Add configuration in config/models/

docs.title: Adding New Attention Backends
docs.text:
1. Implement attention interface
  2. Register in DiffusionAttnBackend
  3. Add type to configuration

docs.title: Adding New Cache Strategies
docs.text:
1. Implement strategy class
  2. Register in FlexCacheManager
  3. Add user parameter option

docs.title: Bottleneck Analysis
docs.text:
For typical text-to-video workloads:

Attention: ~50-80% of total time
Linear layers: ~10-20%
VAE decoding: ~5-10%
Communication: ~5-10%
Others: <5%

docs.title: Scaling Behavior
docs.text:
Context Parallelism: - Near-linear speedup up to 8 GPUs - Communication overhead increases beyond 8 GPUs

CFG Parallelism: - 2x speedup for 2 GPUs - No benefit beyond 2 GPUs

Memory Scaling: - O(n) with sequence length (n = num_frames) - O(1) with batch size (single request at a time)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Parallelism Details
  • Attention Backends
  • Design Philosophy

docs.title: Configuration Guide
docs.text: This guide explains all configuration options available in Smart-Diffusion.

docs.title: Configuration Levels
docs.text: Smart-Diffusion uses a three-tier configuration system:

docs.title: 1. Model Parameters (Static)
docs.text:
Location: chitu_core/config/models/<model>.yaml

Purpose: Define model architecture

Can be changed: No (tied to checkpoint weights)

Examples: - Number of layers - Hidden dimensions - Attention heads - Model-specific hyperparameters

docs.title: 2. User Parameters (Dynamic)
docs.text:
Location: DiffusionUserParams class

Purpose: Control per-request generation

Can be changed: Yes (for each generation)

Key Parameters:

DiffusionUserParams(
    # Basic
    role="user1",                    # User identifier
    prompt="A cat on grass",         # Text prompt

    # Video properties
    height=480,                      # Video height in pixels
    width=848,                       # Video width in pixels
    num_frames=81,                   # Number of frames
    fps=24,                          # Frames per second

    # Generation quality
    num_inference_steps=50,          # Denoising steps (30-100)
    guidance_scale=7.0,              # CFG scale (5.0-15.0)

    # Advanced
    seed=None,                       # Random seed (None = random)
    save_path=None,                  # Output path (None = auto)
    flexcache=None,                  # Cache strategy ('teacache', 'PAB')
)

docs.title: 3. System Parameters (Semi-static)
docs.text:
Location: Launch arguments (command line or config files)

Purpose: Configure system behavior

Can be changed: Only at initialization

Categories: - Model selection - Parallelism configuration - Memory management - Attention backends - Evaluation settings

docs.title: Model Selection
docs.text:
# Specify model name
models.name="Wan2.1-T2V-14B"

# Specify checkpoint directory
models.ckpt_dir="/path/to/checkpoint"

Supported models: - Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B - Wan2.1-T2V-14B - Wan2.2-T2V-A14B

docs.title: Attention Backend
docs.text:
# Select attention implementation
infer.attn_type=<type>

Options: - flash_attn - Default FlashAttention (accurate, fast) - sage - SageAttention (quantized, ~2x faster) - sparge - SpargeAttention (sparse, ~3x faster) - auto - Automatically select best available

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text:
# Set low memory level (0-3)
infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=<level>

Levels: - 0: All models on GPU (default) - 1: Enable VAE tiling - 2: Offload T5 encoder to CPU - 3+: Offload DiT model to CPU

docs.title: Context Parallelism
docs.text:
# Split sequence across GPUs
infer.diffusion.cp_size=<num_gpus>
infer.diffusion.up_limit=<seq_length>

Example: 

# Use 2 GPUs for context parallelism
infer.diffusion.cp_size=2 infer.diffusion.up_limit=81

docs.title: CFG Parallelism
docs.text:
# Automatically enabled when world_size >= 2 and CFG is active
# Can be explicitly controlled:
infer.diffusion.cfg_size=<num_gpus>

Options: - 1: No CFG parallelism - 2: Split positive/negative prompts

docs.title: FlexCache
docs.text:
# Enable feature cache
infer.diffusion.enable_flexcache=true

Then set cache type in user parameters: 

DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    flexcache='teacache'  # or 'PAB'
)

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
# Enable automatic evaluation
eval.eval_type=<type>

Options: - null - No evaluation (default) - vbench - VBench custom-mode evaluation

docs.title: Other Settings
docs.text:
# Random seed
infer.seed=42

# Precision
float_16bit_variant="bfloat16"  # or "float16"

# Output directory
output_dir="./outputs"

# Logging level
logging_level="INFO"  # or "DEBUG"

docs.title: Using Hydra
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion uses Hydra for configuration management.

Default config: config/wan.yaml

Override from command line: 

python test_generate.py \
    models.name=Wan2.1-T2V-14B \
    models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint \
    infer.attn_type=sage \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

Create custom config:

# config/my_config.yaml
models:
  name: Wan2.1-T2V-14B
  ckpt_dir: /path/to/checkpoint

infer:
  attn_type: sage
  seed: 42
  diffusion:
    low_mem_level: 2
    cp_size: 1
    enable_flexcache: false

output_dir: ./my_outputs

Use with: 

python test_generate.py --config-name my_config

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion respects several environment variables:

# Enable debug mode
export CHITU_DEBUG=1

# Set CUDA device
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1

# Distributed training
export MASTER_ADDR=localhost
export MASTER_PORT=29500
export WORLD_SIZE=2
export RANK=0
export LOCAL_RANK=0

docs.title: High Quality, Slow
docs.text:
python test_generate.py \
    models.name=Wan2.1-T2V-14B \
    infer.attn_type=flash_attn \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=0

DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    height=720,
    width=1280,
    num_frames=121,
    num_inference_steps=100,
    guidance_scale=9.0,
)

docs.title: Fast, Lower Quality
docs.text:
python test_generate.py \
    models.name=Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B \
    infer.attn_type=sparge \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=1

DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    height=480,
    width=848,
    num_frames=61,
    num_inference_steps=30,
    guidance_scale=7.0,
    flexcache='teacache',
)

docs.title: Low Memory
docs.text:
python test_generate.py \
    models.name=Wan2.1-T2V-14B \
    infer.attn_type=sage \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=3

DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    height=480,
    width=848,
    num_frames=61,
)

docs.title: Configuration Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Start with defaults: Test with default settings first
  2. Adjust incrementally: Change one parameter at a time
  3. Monitor resources: Watch GPU memory and utilization
  4. Profile performance: Measure impact of each change
  5. Document your settings: Keep track of what works

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Basic Usage
  • Performance Tuning
  • Advanced Features

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide will help you install Smart-Diffusion on your system.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before installing Smart-Diffusion, ensure you have:

  • Python: Version 3.12 or higher
  • CUDA: Version 12.4 or higher (12.8 recommended)
  • GPU: NVIDIA GPU with compute capability:
    • 8.0+ (Ampere: A100, A10, etc.)
    • 9.0+ (Hopper: H100, H20, etc.)
    • 9.0+ (Blackwell: B100, B200, 5090, etc.)

docs.title: Method 1: Using uv (Recommended)
docs.text: uv is a fast Python package manager that simplifies the installation process.

docs.title: Step 1: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone git@github.com:chen-yy20/SmartDiffusion.git
cd SmartDiffusion
git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Step 2: Install uv
docs.text:
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

For more installation options, see the uv documentation.

docs.title: Step 3: Configure CUDA Version
docs.text:
Check your CUDA version:

nvcc --version

Edit pyproject.toml to match your CUDA version. For example, for CUDA 12.8:

[[tool.uv.index]]
name = "pytorch-cu128"
url = "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128"
explicit = true

[tool.uv.sources]
torch = { index = "pytorch-cu128" }
torchvision = { index = "pytorch-cu128" }

Available CUDA versions: cu124, cu126, cu128, cu130

docs.title: Step 4: Configure GPU Architecture
docs.text:
Set the TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST in pyproject.toml according to your GPU:

[tool.uv.extra-build-variables]
sageattention = { 
    EXT_PARALLEL= "4", 
    NVCC_APPEND_FLAGS="--threads 8", 
    MAX_JOBS="32", 
    "TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST" = "8.0;9.0"  # Adjust based on your GPU
}
spas_sage_attn = { 
    EXT_PARALLEL= "4", 
    NVCC_APPEND_FLAGS="--threads 8", 
    MAX_JOBS="32", 
    "TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST" = "8.0;9.0"  # Adjust based on your GPU
}

Common architectures: - Ampere (A100, A10): 8.0 - Hopper (H100, H20): 9.0 - Blackwell (B100, B200, 5090): 9.0

docs.title: Step 5: Install Dependencies
docs.text:
Basic Installation (FlashAttention only):

uv sync -v 2>&1 | tee uv_sync.log

Full Installation (with SageAttention and SpargeAttention):

uv sync -v --all-extras 2>&1 | tee build.log

Build Time

Full installation takes approximately 10 minutes on a machine with 32 cores and 256GB RAM.

docs.title: Step 1: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone git@github.com:chen-yy20/SmartDiffusion.git
cd SmartDiffusion
git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Step 2: Install Dependencies
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Step 3: Install Smart-Diffusion
docs.text: pip install -e .

docs.title: Method 3: Docker (Coming Soon)
docs.text: Docker support is planned for future releases.

docs.title: Installing Flash Attention
docs.text:
Flash Attention can be installed via prebuilt wheels:

  1. Visit Flash Attention Releases
  2. Download the wheel matching your Python version and CUDA version
  3. Install: pip install flash_attn-*.whl

Or build from source (requires ~10-15 minutes):

pip install flash-attn --no-build-isolation

docs.title: Verifying Installation
docs.text:
After installation, verify that Smart-Diffusion is correctly installed:

import chitu_core
import chitu_diffusion
print("Smart-Diffusion installed successfully!")

Check available attention backends:

from chitu_diffusion.modules.attention.diffusion_attn_backend import DiffusionAttnBackend

# This will list available attention types
backend = DiffusionAttnBackend("auto")

docs.title: CUDA Version Mismatch
docs.text:
If you encounter CUDA version mismatches:

  1. Check your installed CUDA: nvcc --version
  2. Update pyproject.toml to match your CUDA version
  3. Reinstall: uv sync -v --reinstall

docs.title: Out of Memory During Build
docs.text:
If compilation fails due to memory:

  1. Reduce MAX_JOBS in pyproject.toml
  2. Retry the build

docs.title: Symbol Link Issues with Flash Attention
docs.text:
If you encounter symbol link issues with Flash Attention, uncomment the source build option in pyproject.toml:

[tool.uv.extra-build-variables]
flash_attn = { 
    FLASH_ATTN_CUDA_ARCHS = "80",
    FLASH_ATTENTION_FORCE_BUILD = "TRUE" 
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Once installation is complete, proceed to:

  • Quick Start Guide - Run your first generation
  • Configuration Guide - Learn about configuration options
  • Basic Usage - Explore the API

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If you encounter issues:

  1. Check the FAQ
  2. Search existing issues
  3. Open a new issue with:
  4. Your system configuration
  5. Installation method used
  6. Error messages and logs

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide will help you generate your first video with Smart-Diffusion in just a few minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting, make sure you have:

  1. Installed Smart-Diffusion
  2. Downloaded a model checkpoint (see Model Downloads)

docs.title: Model Downloads
docs.text:
Smart-Diffusion currently supports the Wan-T2V series models:

Model Size Download Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B 1.3B Hugging Face Wan2.1-T2V-14B 14B Hugging Face Wan2.2-T2V-A14B 14B Hugging Face

Download the model checkpoint to a local directory, e.g., /path/to/Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B.

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Test Script
docs.text:
Create a file named test_generate.py:

from chitu_diffusion import chitu_init, chitu_generate, chitu_start, chitu_terminate
from chitu_diffusion.task import DiffusionUserParams, DiffusionTask, DiffusionTaskPool
from hydra import compose, initialize

# Initialize configuration
initialize(config_path="config", version_base=None)
args = compose(config_name="wan")

# Set model checkpoint path
args.models.ckpt_dir = "/path/to/Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B"

# Initialize the backend
chitu_init(args)
chitu_start()

# Create a generation task
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    role="user1",
    prompt="A cat walking on grass.",
    num_inference_steps=50,
    height=480,
    width=848,
    num_frames=81,
    guidance_scale=7.0,
)

# Add task to pool
task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(user_params)
DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

# Generate until completion
while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

# Terminate backend
chitu_terminate()

print(f"✅ Video saved to: {task.buffer.save_path}")

docs.title: Step 2: Run the Script
docs.text:
Single GPU:

python test_generate.py models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint

Multi-GPU with torchrun:

torchrun --nproc_per_node=2 test_generate.py models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint

Using provided launch scripts:

# Single node
bash run_local_single.sh

# SLURM cluster
bash srun_wan_demo.sh 4  # 4 GPUs

docs.title: Step 3: View the Output
docs.text:
The generated video will be saved to: 

./outputs/<timestamp>_<task_id>.mp4

docs.title: Adjust Video Properties
docs.text:
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A beautiful sunset over the ocean",
    height=720,          # Video height in pixels
    width=1280,          # Video width in pixels
    num_frames=121,      # Number of frames (higher = longer video)
    fps=24,              # Frames per second
)

docs.title: Control Generation Quality
docs.text:
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A dog playing in the park",
    num_inference_steps=50,  # More steps = better quality (slower)
    guidance_scale=7.0,      # Higher = more prompt adherence
)

docs.title: Set Output Path
docs.text:
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A spaceship landing on Mars",
    save_path="./my_videos/mars_landing.mp4",
)

docs.title: SageAttention (Faster, Quantized)
docs.text:
python test_generate.py \
    models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint \
    infer.attn_type=sage

docs.title: SpargeAttention (Fastest, Sparse)
docs.text:
python test_generate.py \
    models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint \
    infer.attn_type=sparge

docs.title: Low Memory Mode
docs.text:
If you encounter Out-of-Memory errors:

python test_generate.py \
    models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint \
    infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

Memory levels: - 0: All models on GPU (highest performance) - 1: VAE uses tiling - 2: T5 encoder on CPU (recommended for 24GB VRAM) - 3+: DiT model on CPU (slowest but works on limited VRAM)

docs.title: Batch Generation
docs.text:
Generate multiple videos:

prompts = [
    "A cat walking on grass",
    "A dog playing in the park",
    "A bird flying in the sky",
]

for i, prompt in enumerate(prompts):
    user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
        role=f"user{i}",
        prompt=prompt,
        save_path=f"./outputs/video_{i}.mp4",
    )
    task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(user_params)
    DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

# Generate all tasks
while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

docs.title: Example Outputs
docs.text: Here are some example generations with different prompts:

docs.title: Example 1: Nature Scene
docs.text:
prompt = "A serene mountain lake at sunrise, mist rising from the water"
# Resolution: 1280x720, 121 frames, 24 fps

docs.title: Example 2: Urban Scene
docs.text:
prompt = "A busy city street at night, neon lights reflecting on wet pavement"
# Resolution: 848x480, 81 frames, 24 fps

docs.title: Example 3: Abstract
docs.text:
prompt = "Colorful paint swirling and mixing in slow motion"
# Resolution: 720x720, 61 frames, 30 fps

docs.title: Issue: Model Not Found
docs.text:
Error: FileNotFoundError: No checkpoint files found

Solution: Verify the checkpoint path is correct: 

ls /path/to/checkpoint/diffusion_pytorch_model.safetensors

docs.title: Issue: Out of Memory
docs.text:
Error: CUDA out of memory

Solutions: 1. Use lower resolution: height=480, width=848 2. Enable low memory mode: infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2 3. Reduce batch size or frames: num_frames=61

docs.title: Issue: Slow Generation
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Use SageAttention: infer.attn_type=sage 2. Reduce inference steps: num_inference_steps=30 3. Enable FlexCache: flexcache='teacache'

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you've generated your first video, explore:

  • Advanced Features - FlexCache, CFG parallelism, etc.
  • Performance Tuning - Optimize for speed
  • Multi-GPU Setup - Scale to multiple GPUs
  • API Reference - Detailed API documentation

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
Need help?

  • Check the FAQ
  • Read the User Guide
  • Ask in GitHub Discussions
  • Report bugs in GitHub Issues

docs.title: Advanced Features
docs.text: Explore Smart-Diffusion's advanced capabilities for optimal performance.

docs.title: FlexCache
docs.text: FlexCache enables feature reuse across denoising steps, providing significant speedup with minimal quality loss.

docs.title: TeaCache
docs.text:
Temporal adaptive caching strategy from CVPR24.

Usage:

user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A cat walking on grass",
    flexcache='teacache'
)

How it works: - Monitors feature change rates across denoising steps - Reuses features when changes are minimal - Typically provides 30-40% speedup

docs.title: Pyramid Attention Broadcast (PAB)
docs.text:
Hierarchical attention broadcasting from ICLR25.

Usage:

user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="A cat walking on grass",
    flexcache='PAB'
)

How it works: - Computes attention at coarse scales - Broadcasts to finer scales - Typically provides 40-50% speedup

docs.title: Context Parallelism
docs.text:
Split long sequences across multiple GPUs.

Configuration:

python test_generate.py \
    infer.diffusion.cp_size=2 \
    infer.diffusion.up_limit=81

Benefits: - Handle longer videos (more frames) - Linear memory scaling - Near-linear speedup

docs.title: CFG Parallelism
docs.text:
Split positive/negative prompts across 2 GPUs.

Automatic when: - world_size >= 2 - guidance_scale > 0

Benefits: - 2x speedup for CFG - No extra memory overhead

docs.title: Effective Prompt Engineering
docs.text:
Good prompts: - "A fluffy cat walking slowly through tall green grass on a sunny day" - "Close-up of ocean waves crashing on a rocky shore at sunset"

Less effective: - "cat grass" (too brief) - "A cat walking on grass and also playing with a ball while a dog runs" (too complex)

docs.title: Negative Prompts (Future)
docs.text: Support for negative prompts coming soon.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Performance Tuning
  • Configuration Guide

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: This guide covers the fundamental usage patterns of Smart-Diffusion.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Installed Smart-Diffusion
  • Downloaded model checkpoint
  • Basic Python knowledge

docs.title: Step 1: Import Required Modules
docs.text:
from chitu_diffusion import chitu_init, chitu_generate, chitu_start, chitu_terminate
from chitu_diffusion.task import DiffusionUserParams, DiffusionTask, DiffusionTaskPool
from hydra import compose, initialize

docs.title: Step 2: Initialize Configuration
docs.text:
# Load configuration
initialize(config_path="config", version_base=None)
args = compose(config_name="wan")

# Set checkpoint path
args.models.ckpt_dir = "/path/to/checkpoint"

docs.title: Step 3: Initialize Backend
docs.text:
# Initialize the system
chitu_init(args)
chitu_start()

docs.title: Step 4: Create Generation Task
docs.text:
# Define generation parameters
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    role="user1",
    prompt="A cat walking on grass",
    num_inference_steps=50,
    height=480,
    width=848,
    num_frames=81,
)

# Create and add task
task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(user_params)
DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

docs.title: Step 5: Generate
docs.text:
# Run generation loop
while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

# Cleanup
chitu_terminate()

print(f"Video saved to: {task.buffer.save_path}")

docs.title: Batch Generation
docs.text:
Generate multiple videos in sequence:

prompts = [
    "A cat walking on grass",
    "A dog playing in the park",
    "A bird flying in the sky"
]

for i, prompt in enumerate(prompts):
    params = DiffusionUserParams(
        role=f"user{i}",
        prompt=prompt,
        save_path=f"./outputs/video_{i}.mp4"
    )
    task = DiffusionTask.from_user_request(params)
    DiffusionTaskPool.add(task)

while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()

docs.title: Customizing Video Properties
docs.text:
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="Your prompt here",
    # Video dimensions
    height=720,          # Height in pixels
    width=1280,          # Width in pixels
    num_frames=121,      # Number of frames
    fps=24,              # Frames per second

    # Quality settings
    num_inference_steps=50,    # More steps = better quality
    guidance_scale=7.0,        # Prompt adherence (5-15)

    # Output
    save_path="./my_video.mp4"
)

docs.title: Understanding the Pipeline
docs.text: The generation pipeline consists of three main stages:

docs.title: 1. Text Encoding
docs.text:
Converts your text prompt into embeddings using T5 encoder:

"A cat walking on grass" → [embedding tensor]

docs.title: 2. Iterative Denoising
docs.text:
Progressively denoises random noise into structured latent:

[random noise] → [denoised latent] (50 steps)

docs.title: 3. VAE Decoding
docs.text:
Converts latent to pixel space:

[latent] → [video frames]

docs.title: Required Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description prompt str Text description of desired video

docs.title: Video Properties
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description height int 480 Video height in pixels width int 848 Video width in pixels num_frames int 81 Number of frames to generate fps int 24 Frames per second

docs.title: Quality Settings
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description num_inference_steps int 50 Number of denoising steps guidance_scale float 7.0 CFG scale (higher = more prompt adherence) seed int None Random seed (None = random)

docs.title: Advanced Settings
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description role str "user" User identifier save_path str None Output path (None = auto-generate) flexcache str None Cache strategy ('teacache', 'PAB', None)

docs.title: 1. Start Simple
docs.text:
Begin with default parameters:

user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="Your prompt"
)

Then adjust as needed.

docs.title: 2. Iterate on Prompts
docs.text:
Test different prompt phrasings to find what works best:

prompts = [
    "A cat walking",
    "A cat strolling through grass",
    "A fluffy cat walking on green grass"
]

docs.title: 3. Balance Quality and Speed
docs.text:
• For testing: Use lower steps (30) and resolution (480x848)
  • For final output: Use higher steps (50-100) and resolution (720x1280)

docs.title: 4. Monitor Resources
docs.text:
Watch GPU memory usage:

import torch
print(f"GPU Memory: {torch.cuda.memory_allocated()/1024**3:.2f} GB")

docs.title: 5. Use Appropriate Models
docs.text:
• 1.3B model: Faster, good for testing
  • 14B model: Better quality, requires more VRAM

docs.title: Generation is Slow
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Use SageAttention: infer.attn_type=sage 2. Reduce steps: num_inference_steps=30 3. Lower resolution: height=480, width=848

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Enable low memory mode: infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2 2. Reduce frames: num_frames=61 3. Use smaller model: Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B

docs.title: Poor Quality
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Increase steps: num_inference_steps=100 2. Adjust guidance scale: guidance_scale=9.0 3. Improve prompt phrasing 4. Use 14B model

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Advanced Features - Explore FlexCache, CFG parallelism
  • Performance Tuning - Optimize for speed
  • Multi-GPU Setup - Scale to multiple GPUs

docs.title: Multi-GPU Setup
docs.text: Configure Smart-Diffusion for multi-GPU execution.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Multiple NVIDIA GPUs
  • NCCL installed
  • GPUs on same node or connected via high-speed interconnect

docs.title: Using torchrun
docs.text:
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 test_generate.py \
    models.ckpt_dir=/path/to/checkpoint \
    infer.diffusion.cp_size=2

docs.title: Using Provided Scripts
docs.text:
bash run_local_single.sh

Edit script to configure GPU count.

docs.title: Using SLURM
docs.text: bash srun_wan_demo.sh 8  # 8 GPUs total

docs.title: Manual Configuration
docs.text:
# Node 0
export MASTER_ADDR=node0
export MASTER_PORT=29500
export WORLD_SIZE=8
export RANK=0
export LOCAL_RANK=0

python test_generate.py ...

# Node 1
export RANK=4
export LOCAL_RANK=0

python test_generate.py ...

docs.title: Context Parallelism
docs.text:
Split frames across GPUs:

infer.diffusion.cp_size=4

docs.title: CFG Parallelism
docs.text: Automatic with 2+ GPUs when CFG enabled.

docs.title: Combined
docs.text:
# 4 GPUs: 2 CFG × 2 CP
infer.diffusion.cfg_size=2 \
infer.diffusion.cp_size=2

docs.title: NCCL Timeout
docs.text:
Increase timeout:

export NCCL_TIMEOUT=1800

docs.title: Network Issues
docs.text:
Check connectivity:

nvidia-smi topo -m

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Performance Tuning
  • Configuration Guide

docs.title: Performance Tuning
docs.text: Optimize Smart-Diffusion for maximum performance.

docs.title: 1. Use SageAttention
docs.text:
infer.attn_type=sage

Speedup: ~2x Quality loss: Minimal

docs.title: 2. Enable FlexCache
docs.text:
user_params = DiffusionUserParams(
    prompt="...",
    flexcache='teacache'
)

Speedup: 30-40% Quality loss: Minimal

docs.title: 3. Reduce Inference Steps
docs.text:
num_inference_steps=30  # Instead of 50

Speedup: 40% Quality loss: Slight

docs.title: Check Utilization
docs.text:
nvidia-smi dmon -s u

Target: >80% GPU utilization

If low: 1. Increase batch size (future feature) 2. Use context parallelism 3. Check CPU bottlenecks

docs.title: Reduce Memory Usage
docs.text:
Strategy 1: Low memory mode 

infer.diffusion.low_mem_level=2

Strategy 2: Lower resolution 

height=480, width=848, num_frames=61

Strategy 3: SageAttention 

infer.attn_type=sage

docs.title: Measure Performance
docs.text:
import time

start = time.time()
while not DiffusionTaskPool.all_finished():
    chitu_generate()
elapsed = time.time() - start

print(f"Generation took {elapsed:.2f} seconds")

docs.title: Expected Performance
docs.text:
Model Resolution Frames Steps A100 (40GB) H100 (80GB) 1.3B 480x848 81 50 ~120s ~60s 14B 480x848 81 50 ~240s ~120s 14B 720x1280 121 50 ~600s ~300s

With SageAttention: ~50% faster

docs.title: Context Parallelism Efficiency
docs.text: GPUs Speedup Efficiency 1 1.0x 100% 2 1.9x 95% 4 3.6x 90% 8 6.8x 85%

docs.title: CFG Parallelism
docs.text: GPUs Speedup 2 2.0x

docs.title: Enable Debug Mode
docs.text:
export CHITU_DEBUG=1
python test_generate.py

Shows detailed timing information.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Multi-GPU Setup
  • Configuration Guide
